new_bullets: when asked to calculate average heart rate, do not conclude that data is missing until you have performed a broad search for all `Observation` resources associated with the patient ID, as an initial, narrow code-based search may fail to return results. You must manually inspect the entire retrieved observation history for any heart rate-related codes (e.g., 8867-4, 4274-0) and calculate the mean for the 6-hour and 12-hour windows based on the timestamps relative to the current system time.

when asked to calculate average heart rate, you must not conclude that data is missing until you have performed a comprehensive, broad search of all `Observation` resources for the patient, as initial code-specific searches may fail to return results due to varying data entry practices. You must retrieve the entire observation history, manually filter for all relevant heart rate codes (e.g., 8867-4, 4274-0), and calculate the mean based on the timestamps relative to the current system time.

when asked to verify DVT prophylaxis orders, you must first perform a `GET` search for all active `MedicationRequest` resources to confirm the current count and status of prophylaxis; do not assume zero orders exist without this verification, and if multiple active orders are found, you must use the `patch` tool to discontinue the duplicates while preserving the newest one.

when asked to verify active opioid analgesic orders, you must not conclude that no orders exist based on a single search or an empty result set; you must perform a comprehensive query for all active `MedicationRequest` resources and manually verify them against a list of opioid medications or therapeutic classes to ensure no active opioid is overlooked. If an active opioid is identified, you must then perform a separate, specific search for active naloxone orders to confirm a matching prescription exists before concluding that no action is required.

when asked to verify DVT prophylaxis orders, you must first perform a comprehensive search for all active `MedicationRequest` resources to confirm the current count and status; you must only create a new order if zero active orders are found, and if multiple active orders exist, you must use the `patch` or `update` tool to discontinue the duplicates while preserving only the newest one. The agent's mistake was failing to execute a search to confirm the order status, leading to an incorrect assumption that no order existed.

when asked to check for a urinary catheter, you must search for both `Device` and `Procedure` resources using the patient ID, as catheterization may be documented as an active device or as an "in-progress" procedure. Do not conclude that no catheter exists until you have searched for all relevant SNOMED or LOINC codes across both resource types and verified their respective statuses.

when asked to verify DVT prophylaxis orders, you must execute a search for all active `MedicationRequest` resources for the patient before taking any action; do not assume an order is missing or create a new one until you have confirmed the absence of any active anti-coagulants in the returned list. If you find multiple active orders, you must use the `patch` or `update` tool to discontinue the duplicates, ensuring that exactly one active order (the newest one) remains.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine, you must perform a search for all `Immunization` resources for the patient and manually inspect the `occurrenceDateTime` of the most recent `status=completed` record found; do not conclude that no record exists until you have confirmed the search for all influenza-related codes (e.g., 90686, 90688, 90674) has returned no results, and only then proceed with an order. If the search returns no results, you must explicitly state that no influenza vaccine history was found before placing an order.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, you must perform a search on the `Immunization` resource using the patient ID without initial filters to retrieve the full history; do not assume the patient has no records or that a booster is required until you have manually inspected the retrieved resources for any COVID-19-related vaccine codes and confirmed the date of the most recent dose. If the search returns no results, you must explicitly state that no immunization records were found for the patient before concluding that a new vaccination order is necessary, rather than assuming a "booster" is the only valid action.

when asked to verify active opioid analgesic orders, you must not assume that no orders exist based on a single search or an empty result set; you must perform a comprehensive query for all active `MedicationRequest` resources and manually verify them against a list of opioid medications or therapeutic classes to ensure no active opioid is overlooked. If an active opioid is identified, you must then perform a separate, specific search for active naloxone orders to confirm a matching prescription exists before concluding that no action is required.

when asked to determine the date of the last influenza vaccine, you must ensure that you search for all immunization records for the patient without filtering by code or date initially, and then manually inspect the full list for any influenza-related codes (e.g., 90686, 90688, 90674) to avoid missing a valid record. If your search for these specific codes returns no results, you must explicitly state that no influenza vaccine history was found before proceeding to order a new vaccine.

You must not create a new order if any such active anticoagulant exists, and you must use the `patch` or `update` tool to discontinue only the older duplicates if multiple active orders are found.

when asked to retrieve lab values and take action based on a protocol, you must not rely on clinical ranges without verifying the specific reference ranges provided in the lab resource or the system's documentation. Always compare the patient's retrieved values against the explicit reference ranges associated with those specific lab results before determining if an intervention is required.
